Focused Story

This fixture keeps the article body clearly readable while remaining smaller than the surrounding chrome if stop selectors are ignored.

Another paragraph keeps the article above the scoped-root confidence threshold and makes the root choice observable.

Why This Matters

The article body needs enough semantic structure and text density to qualify as the real reading surface even when the surrounding main container contains a large discussion block.

That makes this regression test meaningful instead of passing only because the article itself fails the confidence gate.

What To Expect

The generic selector set should still resolve to the article because the stop selectors mark the comment subtree as non-content chrome for scoring purposes.

A final paragraph closes the loop so the page still looks like a realistic reading surface.

Comment 1: this discussion block is intentionally long and repetitive so the enclosing main container would otherwise outscore the actual article root.

Comment 2: this discussion block is intentionally long and repetitive so the enclosing main container would otherwise outscore the actual article root.
